skill.name: medical-case-interpreter
skill.description: Generates compliant medical case report articles for WeChat.
skill.summary: Transforms raw clinical data into structured, anonymized medical case reports for WeChat publication.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 32 potential issues across 3 files, but evaluation confirmed all findings are false positives in context. This skill is a legitimate medical content generation template that transforms clinical data into formatted WeChat case reports with built-in anonymization. The referenced script (scripts/validate_skill.py) is a minimal argparse stub with no dangerous operations. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, command injection, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at scripts/validate_skill.py:12 and SKILL.md:3,19,58,110,123. All locations contain plain text descriptions and argparse configuration strings, not cryptographic operations. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at medical-case-interpreter_audit_result_v2.json:260 and SKILL.md:46. Both locations contain plain text evaluation notes and documentation. No system reconnaissance behavior detected. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 22 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All instances are markdown code formatting backticks used for inline code (`Python`, `3.10+`) and fenced code blocks (```bash). Markdown formatting characters are not shell execution. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a 'hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. This is a standard GitHub source attribution link referencing the skill's repository. Legitimate and expected for community-published skills. FALSE POSITIVE.

content.user_title: Generate Compliant Medical Case Reports for WeChat
content.value_statement: This skill transforms raw clinical data into professional, compliant medical case report articles formatted for WeChat publication. It enforces strict patient privacy protections and structured formatting rules to produce consistent, reviewable medical content every time.
actual_capabilities: Transforms raw clinical text data into formatted medical case report articles ready for WeChat
Enforces patient privacy through automatic anonymization of names, dates, and hospital identifiers
Generates structured sections: Patient Information, Auxiliary Examination, Diagnosis, and Treatment Process
Highlights specific drug or product names within the treatment narrative
Supports optional Chinese drug name mapping for localized content
Produces a deterministic, reviewable output with a validation summary
limitations: Cannot access external medical databases or verify clinical facts against external sources
Requires complete clinical data input and cannot fabricate or infer missing information
Output is optimized for WeChat article format and may need adaptation for other platforms
Does not batch process multiple cases in a single execution

use_cases.title: Medical Affairs Publishing
use_cases.description: Medical affairs teams convert internal clinical case studies into formatted WeChat articles for educational outreach to healthcare professionals.

use_cases.title: Pharmaceutical Content Marketing
use_cases.description: Marketing teams transform clinical data into compliant, doctor-facing case reports that highlight product benefits within regulatory guidelines.

use_cases.title: Clinical Education Documentation
use_cases.description: Researchers and physicians document interesting patient cases for training and education, ensuring consistent formatting and privacy compliance.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Single-Drug Case Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Please generate a case report for [Product Name] based on the attached clinical data. The doctor is [Doctor Name].
prompt_templates.scenario: User has patient data and needs a quick formatted case report with minimal configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Case Report with Drug Name Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a case report for [Product Name] using the attached patient file. The doctor is [Doctor Name]. Use the following Chinese drug name mapping: [Drug Mapping Details].
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs Chinese translations for drug names to maintain consistency with local medical terminology.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Condition Case Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a detailed case report for [Product Name] from the attached clinical file. Doctor: [Doctor Name]. The patient has multiple conditions. Include all examination results and clearly differentiate each treatment phase. Drug Mapping: [Mapping Details].
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex patient case with multiple conditions and treatment phases requiring thorough documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Expert Report with Custom Section Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the attached clinical file, produce a case report for [Product Name]. Doctor: [Doctor Name]. Apply drug mapping: [Mapping Details]. Add a clinical discussion section after the case body. Keep the foreword under 150 words. Use professional medical terminology throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Senior medical writer needs additional analysis sections with strict length constraints and specialized formatting.

output_examples.input: 45-year-old male patient, admitted with chest pain. ECG showed ST elevation in leads V1-V4. Troponin I elevated to 2.5 ng/mL. Treated with CardioProtect 75mg daily.
output: ### Patient Information: Patient (Male, 45) presented with acute chest pain. No prior cardiac history reported.
### Auxiliary Examination: ECG showed significant ST elevation in leads V1-V4. Troponin I elevated to 2.5 ng/mL.
### Diagnosis: Acute Anterior ST-Elevation Myocardial Infarction
### Treatment Process: Patient was started on CardioProtect 75mg daily immediately after diagnosis.
### Summary: Patient showed positive response to treatment with symptom resolution within 48 hours.

output_examples.input: 8-year-old female patient with persistent cough for 3 weeks. Spirometry showed FEV1/FVC ratio of 0.70. Diagnosed with asthma. Treated with RespCare inhaler.
output: ### Patient Information: Patient (Female, 8) presented with persistent non-productive cough lasting 3 weeks.
### Auxiliary Examination: Spirometry showed FEV1/FVC ratio of 0.70, indicating obstructive airway pattern.
### Treatment Process: Inhaled corticosteroid therapy initiated with RespCare 100mcg twice daily.
### Summary: Patient showed symptom improvement at 2-week follow-up with reduced cough frequency.

best_practices: Provide all available clinical data in a single submission to ensure the complete case history is included in the report.
Use the drug mapping option for Chinese-language WeChat articles to maintain consistency with local medical naming conventions.
Review the anonymization step manually after generation to confirm no protected health information remains in the output.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to fabricate lab results, measurements, or treatment outcomes not supported by the input data.
Do not use this skill to request identifiable patient information in the output, which would violate privacy rules.
Do not use this skill for diagnostic decision-making, clinical treatment recommendations, or regulatory submissions.

faq.question: What types of medical data can this skill process?
faq.answer: It processes text-based clinical data including patient history, examination results, diagnoses, and treatment plans provided by the user.

faq.question: Does this skill access external medical databases?
faq.answer: No. The skill works only with the data you provide. It does not verify clinical facts against external sources.

faq.question: How does this skill protect patient privacy?
faq.answer: It converts patient names to anonymized labels such as Patient (Gender, Age) and replaces specific dates with relative time markers.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for languages other than Chinese and English?
faq.answer: The skill supports English patient data and offers optional Chinese drug name mapping. Other languages are not explicitly supported.

faq.question: What output format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The output is a structured medical case report in markdown format, organized with clear section headers and suitable for WeChat publication.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for regulatory submissions?
faq.answer: No. This skill produces educational and marketing content. It should not be used for regulatory submissions, clinical decision-making, or patient treatment recommendations.
